impact: When a review fails because a Codex CLI internal subsystem (e.g., codex_core::memories::phase1, which hardcodes gpt-5.1-codex-mini) hits a model-unsupported error, our classifier extracts the offending model name correctly from the raw error, but the "Fall back to gpt-5.4" tip is hardcoded and doesn't consider whether the user is ALREADY on gpt-5.4 (or on a completely different model than the extracted failing one). Users see "Fall back to gpt-5.4 by setting \"model\": \"gpt-5.4\" in .reviewbridge.json" when they've already configured gpt-5.4 and the actual failure is from a Codex internal model they cannot reach. The extracted model name in the error message is also confusing — it names a model the user never configured. Reproduced via real debug session on 2026-04-23: review configured for gpt-5.4 failed because Codex CLI's memory-writing agent fired a separate call using gpt-5.1-codex-mini (hardcoded in the Rust binary) and that specific model is unavailable on ChatGPT-subscription tier. Related to ISS-001 in shape — classifier surfacing an unhelpful hint because it lacks context about which layer the model name came from.
resolution: Mitigated by SDK bump in commit 306b4c1 (1.0.0 release): the bundled CLI moved from 0.104.0 to 0.128.0, which no longer exhibits the memory-agent gpt-5.1-codex-mini failure path against ChatGPT-tier auth — the misleading tip stops appearing in the most common scenario. Status remains open because (a) we have not directly probed the memory-agent path under the new binary across all auth/config combinations, and (b) the classifier text is still imprecise if any future Codex subsystem surfaces a model name that differs from the configured one. Future work would tighten the classifier to detect extracted-model ≠ configured-model and emit a distinct tip; tracked separately and not blocking 1.0.0.
title: MODEL_ERROR tip misleads when failure is from Codex internal subsystem (not caller's model)
